_comment: Breadcrumbs seed data. F-093 must be pre-loaded before the demo (Appendix A, Breadcrumbs.pdf). Load it with: python ingestion/write_findings.py schema/seed_findings.json
_provenance_note: F-093 is a fictional pre-existing internal finding invented for the demo, not something actually extracted from a real K Pro chat. write_findings.py requires source_session_id to reference a real, already-ingested chat_sessions row, so it points at 54ecc674-7485-4a18-ac95-a3be5f233ec7 (title 'Default') purely to satisfy that foreign key -- that session is not the origin of this finding's content.

findings.hypothesis_text: Immune/cytotoxic signature as a stage-independent prognostic model
findings.author: Researcher C
findings.provenance: TCGA LUAD, multivariate Cox with stage adjustment
findings.reason: Effect collapsed after stage adjustment - signal tracked tumor stage, not independent biology
findings.note: Do not re-run without multivariate, stage-adjusted design

_live_write_template_F118.purpose: F-118 is NOT pre-seeded -- it is written live in Beat 1 of the demo via the write_finding MCP tool, not this seed script. This shows its shape with {{ }} placeholders to be filled from the real TCGA LUAD run (Appendix B) once survival-analysis produces real numbers. Do not add this object to the 'findings' array above.

example.hypothesis_text: High cytotoxic T-cell infiltration is associated with better overall survival
example.author: Researcher A
example.source_session_id: <the real Session-1 chat id, once that session is ingested>
example.provenance: TCGA LUAD, cBioPortal, Kaplan-Meier + log-rank, median OS high={{MEDIAN_HIGH}}mo vs low={{MEDIAN_LOW}}mo
example.note: Written live during Beat 1 of the demo - do not pre-seed

_optional_second_seed.purpose: Only add if it strengthens (not clutters) the demo - one unrelated confirmed finding in a different indication so the graph visibly holds more than just the demo path.

example.hypothesis_text: STK11 mutation status is associated with worse overall survival
example.author: Researcher B
example.provenance: TCGA LUSC, cBioPortal, Cox proportional hazards
